node.title: Particl is Officially Moving to Discord
node.plaintext:
Better Community Support & More Fun
The Particl community has been spread across many different platforms ever since
we abandoned Slack more than a year ago. This exile away from Slack was due to
the overwhelming amount of spam we used to receive on the platform, and since
that time, the community has been split in between using Telegram, Discord, and
Riot — with our trusty bot “Rutherford” connecting all of the conversations
together in real time.

While it is accommodating, this setup isn’t ideal and it also carries its load
of pickles. For one, it makes spam more time-consuming to deal with, each spam
post is required to be deleted on all three platforms. It also makes
interactions and communications less interesting due to how posts from other
platforms are displayed and formatted. Then there’s the issue of always being
required to maintain the Rutherford bot which tends to break from time to time.

On top of this, and perhaps more importantly, there’s the inconvenience where we
can’t really make the chatrooms as awesome as they could be by using bots since
everyone’s on different platforms; bots sometimes don’t communicate or simply
cannot interact, by nature, with users on different platforms.

For these and other reasons, we’ve made the decision to officially move over to
Discord and methodically reduce resources on Riot and Telegram slowly over time. 
In 7 days, starting from today, the Telegram channels will be put in read-only
mode. This should incentivize the remaining Telegram users to move over while
still allowing new Telegram users to discover the project and read the
conversations.

Particl - Discord Me [https://discord.me/particl]
Official server for the Particl project. Particl is a blockchain project
building a new independent and decentralized… [https://discord.me/particl]
discord.me [https://discord.me/particl]

What Will Improve?
The time is right to move exclusively to Discord. With the release of the Open
Marketplace being imminent, we anticipate an uptick in new users joining our
awesome community. Serving new users on one platform instead of many will make
everyone’s user experience much better and will allow us to do much more. Here’s
a quick list of what the move to Discord will look like and — more
importantly — what opportunities it opens up. Note that this list is obviously
not complete, and new stuff will be added as we march forward.

Better User Experience
This is probably the most obvious benefit we’ll get from moving exclusively to
one platform. Interacting on many different platforms at the same time
definitely reduces the user experience by making much less appealing and,
frankly, more confusing — especially to newcomers coming into our community and
not being used to see bots responding instead of real people.

Let’s face it, linking platforms has many downsides. Bot conversations are
confusing chains of replies to users on different platforms, images struggle
uploading properly everywhere at the same time. The various different Particl
channels, each with their unique URL and mixed between other crypto channels on
Telegram is also pretty chaotic.

Discord will fix all of the above by providing a clean and structured interface
that will go a long way in enhancing the quality of interactions and make
everything more fun and intuitive.

Fight Off Spammers
Spam on Telegram is off the rails!

Every day, we see fake accounts joining the many channels and spamming them with
seriously questionable content or “business proposals”. And it’s not like we
don’t fight back. Even though our Telegram channels have three different bots
specifically dedicated to filtering these kinds of unwanted posts, spam still
happens at a pretty regular rate.

Discord simply offers more tools and ways to fight off spam and oddly seems to
have fewer spammers going around from server to server with the hopes of
deceiving users or just ruin their experience. We’ve also heard very positive
and unanimous feedback from the people from other communities we’ve talked to
that have made “the move” from Telegram to Discord as well: moving over to
Discord has significantly reduced spam and increased community engagement and
participation rates.

Better Ways to Keep Up-To-Date
Another neat benefit the community will gain from being united under the same
platform is the ability to quickly stay up-to-date with the project and get
additional information and content. Because it is very easy to add and find (as
a user) new channels, we’ll be capitalizing on that to keep you all in the loop.

Don’t have Twitter? Can’t be bothered looking around for updates? Forgot to put
Particl’s next event in your calendar? Well, it doesn’t matter anymore, because
we’ve got your back!

In fact, we’ve already created a few channels specifically dedicated to making
your life easier. To be more precise, we’ve created a whole section dedicated to
making project information and updates as accessible as it’s ever been! This
section contains these sub-channels:

 * #announcements: Get all the latest news about Particl. This is usually what
   goes on particl.news.
 * #progress-updates: This sub-channel contains all the development updates and
   general information about the progress being made on the project. This is
   where you’ll find Status Reports, notable Github updates, development
   updates, and etc.
 * #tweets: This section automatically shows all the tweets made from the
   official @ParticlProject Twitter account. It doesn’t matter if you have a
   Twitter account or not, you should still be able to easily get access to our
   tweets if you so desire!
 * #team: Here you will get the chance to learn a bit more about the Particl
   team members by reading various bios and information tidbits about them.
 * #graphics: There’s a lot of Particl-related graphics, but somehow they’ve
   always been hard to get a hold of. Not anymore! This sub-channel contains all
   the latest important graphics. If you are looking for a specific graphic in
   particular, well then you now know where to-3.24% go look for it!
 * #events: This is like your very own Particl calendar. This sub-channel will
   remind you of any Particl-related event 30, 7, and 1 day before they happen.
   This includes conferences, community gatherings, hardforks, release dates,
   and etc. Never miss an event anymore!
 * #youtube: This one is self-explanatory. Just like #tweets, this sub-channel
   will automatically post the latest videos published on Particl’s Youtube
   account.

As time goes by, and as we receive feedback and suggestions, new sub-channels
will be added to make the Particl Discord server the ultimate platform to be on
to not miss anything.

Got Some Spare Change?
We will shortly introduce on Discord an open-source tipping bot that will allow
you to securely send PART tips to other people directly from Discord. This
should make newcomers warmer and more engaged to our community and encourage
people who receive tips to pay it forward, strengthening the bond between us
all!

Automation Make Everything Easier and More Fun
Automation and bots on Discord are the bomb! They are much more flexible than on
Telegram, opening up more opportunities. They allow polls, custom commands,
auto-moderation, music streaming, tags, auto-responders, and much more. This
will allow for many more interactions and much more fun and will help make
Particl’s Discord our very own world.

While we had bots on Telegram and Discord before, we couldn’t really use them to
their full extent as they lose their effectiveness when users are spread across
various different platforms. They obviously cannot interact with users on other
platforms, so we couldn’t make use of their more exciting, fun, and engaging
capabilities. This is poised to change now!

A Better Structure for Community Involvement
We’re also currently working on setting up a better structure for the onboarding
of community members who want to take a more active role in contributing to the
project. As can be easily observed, staying organized when only using a chat
platform can be quite difficult if you do not use any sort of task management
platform.

Using automation, we are in the process of linking a Trello board to a
contributor’s channel to make it easy and accessible to manage tasks. Once
ready, contributors will be able to interact with the Trello board directly from
the channel and collaborate with fellow community members up for the task. This
contributor channel should be ready in a couple of days and will be an
invite-only channel, so don’t hesitate to contact @CryptoGuard#3505 on Discord
so that he can add you to the channel!


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

What Will Become of Rutherford?
Rutherford BotRutherford is the bot that’s been linking all of Particl’s chat
platforms together. To incentivize people to move over to Discord, we will
deactivate it on Telegram and put the platform in a read-only mode in just 7
days, meaning you will be able to read what people say on Discord, but won’t be
able to post messages.

What About Riot?
Riot is a different beast and will get a different treatment with this move to
Discord, at least in the beginning. It doesn’t attract spammers, is relatively
secure by allowing end-to-end encryption, and is where most of our developers
hang around. For these reasons, we will keep Riot and Discord connected for the
moment and will see how it plays out. Due to the low number of community members
on Riot, this shouldn’t impact the user experience too much. Still, we recommend
users looking for a more interactive experience to move over to Discord.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Officially moving to Discord has been something we’ve been looking into doing
for quite some time now, and with the marketplace looming and the potential
uptick in new users it may bring, it only seems logical to make this move now.
We are extremely excited by the new possibilities having our entire community
gathered within the same platform opens up, and we fully intend on making the
best out of this situation to enhance your community experience.

So come to join us today on Discord [https://discord.me/particl] and let the fun
begin!

The Particl team


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Official server for the Particl project. Particl is a blockchain project building a new independent and decentralized…discord.me

What Will Improve?

The time is right to move exclusively to Discord. With the release of the Open Marketplace being imminent, we anticipate an uptick in new users joining our awesome community. Serving new users on one platform instead of many will make everyone’s user experience much better and will allow us to do much more. Here’s a quick list of what the move to Discord will look like and — more importantly — what opportunities it opens up. Note that this list is obviously not complete, and new stuff will be added as we march forward.

Better User Experience

This is probably the most obvious benefit we’ll get from moving exclusively to one platform. Interacting on many different platforms at the same time definitely reduces the user experience by making much less appealing and, frankly, more confusing — especially to newcomers coming into our community and not being used to see bots responding instead of real people.

Let’s face it, linking platforms has many downsides. Bot conversations are confusing chains of replies to users on different platforms, images struggle uploading properly everywhere at the same time. The various different Particl channels, each with their unique URL and mixed between other crypto channels on Telegram is also pretty chaotic.

Discord will fix all of the above by providing a clean and structured interface that will go a long way in enhancing the quality of interactions and make everything more fun and intuitive.

Fight Off Spammers

Spam on Telegram is off the rails!

Every day, we see fake accounts joining the many channels and spamming them with seriously questionable content or “business proposals”. And it’s not like we don’t fight back. Even though our Telegram channels have three different bots specifically dedicated to filtering these kinds of unwanted posts, spam still happens at a pretty regular rate.

Discord simply offers more tools and ways to fight off spam and oddly seems to have fewer spammers going around from server to server with the hopes of deceiving users or just ruin their experience. We’ve also heard very positive and unanimous feedback from the people from other communities we’ve talked to that have made “the move” from Telegram to Discord as well: moving over to Discord has significantly reduced spam and increased community engagement and participation rates.

Better Ways to Keep Up-To-Date

Another neat benefit the community will gain from being united under the same platform is the ability to quickly stay up-to-date with the project and get additional information and content. Because it is very easy to add and find (as a user) new channels, we’ll be capitalizing on that to keep you all in the loop.

Don’t have Twitter? Can’t be bothered looking around for updates? Forgot to put Particl’s next event in your calendar? Well, it doesn’t matter anymore, because we’ve got your back!

In fact, we’ve already created a few channels specifically dedicated to making your life easier. To be more precise, we’ve created a whole section dedicated to making project information and updates as accessible as it’s ever been! This section contains these sub-channels:

  • #announcements: Get all the latest news about Particl. This is usually what goes on particl.news.
  • #progress-updates: This sub-channel contains all the development updates and general information about the progress being made on the project. This is where you’ll find Status Reports, notable Github updates, development updates, and etc.
  • #tweets: This section automatically shows all the tweets made from the official @ParticlProject Twitter account. It doesn’t matter if you have a Twitter account or not, you should still be able to easily get access to our tweets if you so desire!
  • #team: Here you will get the chance to learn a bit more about the Particl team members by reading various bios and information tidbits about them.
  • #graphics: There’s a lot of Particl-related graphics, but somehow they’ve always been hard to get a hold of. Not anymore! This sub-channel contains all the latest important graphics. If you are looking for a specific graphic in particular, well then you now know where to-3.24% go look for it!
  • #events: This is like your very own Particl calendar. This sub-channel will remind you of any Particl-related event 30, 7, and 1 day before they happen. This includes conferences, community gatherings, hardforks, release dates, and etc. Never miss an event anymore!
  • #youtube: This one is self-explanatory. Just like #tweets, this sub-channel will automatically post the latest videos published on Particl’s Youtube account.

As time goes by, and as we receive feedback and suggestions, new sub-channels will be added to make the Particl Discord server the ultimate platform to be on to not miss anything.

Got Some Spare Change?

We will shortly introduce on Discord an open-source tipping bot that will allow you to securely send PART tips to other people directly from Discord. This should make newcomers warmer and more engaged to our community and encourage people who receive tips to pay it forward, strengthening the bond between us all!

Automation Make Everything Easier and More Fun

Automation and bots on Discord are the bomb! They are much more flexible than on Telegram, opening up more opportunities. They allow polls, custom commands, auto-moderation, music streaming, tags, auto-responders, and much more. This will allow for many more interactions and much more fun and will help make Particl’s Discord our very own world.

While we had bots on Telegram and Discord before, we couldn’t really use them to their full extent as they lose their effectiveness when users are spread across various different platforms. They obviously cannot interact with users on other platforms, so we couldn’t make use of their more exciting, fun, and engaging capabilities. This is poised to change now!

A Better Structure for Community Involvement

We’re also currently working on setting up a better structure for the onboarding of community members who want to take a more active role in contributing to the project. As can be easily observed, staying organized when only using a chat platform can be quite difficult if you do not use any sort of task management platform.

Using automation, we are in the process of linking a Trello board to a contributor’s channel to make it easy and accessible to manage tasks. Once ready, contributors will be able to interact with the Trello board directly from the channel and collaborate with fellow community members up for the task. This contributor channel should be ready in a couple of days and will be an invite-only channel, so don’t hesitate to contact @CryptoGuard#3505 on Discord so that he can add you to the channel!

What Will Become of Rutherford?

Rutherford is the bot that’s been linking all of Particl’s chat platforms together. To incentivize people to move over to Discord, we will deactivate it on Telegram and put the platform in a read-only mode in just 7 days, meaning you will be able to read what people say on Discord, but won’t be able to post messages.

What About Riot?

Riot is a different beast and will get a different treatment with this move to Discord, at least in the beginning. It doesn’t attract spammers, is relatively secure by allowing end-to-end encryption, and is where most of our developers hang around. For these reasons, we will keep Riot and Discord connected for the moment and will see how it plays out. Due to the low number of community members on Riot, this shouldn’t impact the user experience too much. Still, we recommend users looking for a more interactive experience to move over to Discord.

Officially moving to Discord has been something we’ve been looking into doing for quite some time now, and with the marketplace looming and the potential uptick in new users it may bring, it only seems logical to make this move now. We are extremely excited by the new possibilities having our entire community gathered within the same platform opens up, and we fully intend on making the best out of this situation to enhance your community experience.

So come to join us today on Discord and let the fun begin!

The Particl team

In fact, we’ve already created a few channels specifically dedicated to making your life easier. To be more precise, we’ve created a whole section dedicated to making project information and updates as accessible as it’s ever been! This section contains these sub-channels:
#announcements:
Get all the latest news about Particl. This is usually what goes on particl.news.
#progress-updates:

This sub-channel contains all the development updates and general information about the progress being made on the project. This is where you’ll find Status Reports, notable Github updates, development updates, and etc.
#tweets:

This section automatically shows all the tweets made from the official @ParticlProject Twitter account. It doesn’t matter if you have a Twitter account or not, you should still be able to easily get access to our tweets if you so desire!
#team:
Here you will get the chance to learn a bit more about the Particl team members by reading various bios and information tidbits about them.
#graphics:

There’s a lot of Particl-related graphics, but somehow they’ve always been hard to get a hold of. Not anymore! This sub-channel contains all the latest important graphics. If you are looking for a specific graphic in particular, well then you now know where to-3.24% go look for it!
#events:

This is like your very own Particl calendar. This sub-channel will remind you of any Particl-related event 30, 7, and 1 day before they happen. This includes conferences, community gatherings, hardforks, release dates, and etc. Never miss an event anymore!
#youtube:
This one is self-explanatory. Just like
#tweets
, this sub-channel will automatically post the latest videos published on Particl’s Youtube account.
As time goes by, and as we receive feedback and suggestions, new sub-channels will be added to make the Particl Discord server the ultimate platform to be on to not miss anything.
Got Some Spare Change?

We will shortly introduce on Discord an open-source tipping bot that will allow you to securely send PART tips to other people directly from Discord. This should make newcomers warmer and more engaged to our community and encourage people who receive tips to pay it forward, strengthening the bond between us all!
Automation Make Everything Easier and More Fun

Automation and bots on Discord are the bomb! They are much more flexible than on Telegram, opening up more opportunities. They allow polls, custom commands, auto-moderation, music streaming, tags, auto-responders, and much more. This will allow for many more interactions and much more fun and will help make Particl’s Discord our very own world.

While we had bots on Telegram and Discord before, we couldn’t really use them to their full extent as they lose their effectiveness when users are spread across various different platforms. They obviously cannot interact with users on other platforms, so we couldn’t make use of their more exciting, fun, and engaging capabilities. This is poised to change now!
A Better Structure for Community Involvement

We’re also currently working on setting up a better structure for the onboarding of community members who want to take a more active role in contributing to the project. As can be easily observed, staying organized when only using a chat platform can be quite difficult if you do not use any sort of task management platform.

Using automation, we are in the process of linking a Trello board to a contributor’s channel to make it easy and accessible to manage tasks. Once ready, contributors will be able to interact with the Trello board directly from the channel and collaborate with fellow community members up for the task. This contributor channel should be ready in a couple of days and will be an invite-only channel, so don’t hesitate to contact
@CryptoGuard#3505
on Discord so that he can add you to the channel!
What Will Become of Rutherford?
Rutherford Bot
Rutherford is the bot that’s been linking all of Particl’s chat platforms together. To incentivize people to move over to Discord, we will deactivate it on Telegram and
put the platform in a read-only mode in just 7 days
, meaning you will be able to read what people say on Discord, but won’t be able to post messages.
What About Riot?

Riot is a different beast and will get a different treatment with this move to Discord, at least in the beginning. It doesn’t attract spammers, is relatively secure by allowing end-to-end encryption, and is where most of our developers hang around. For these reasons, we will keep Riot and Discord connected for the moment and will see how it plays out. Due to the low number of community members on Riot, this shouldn’t impact the user experience too much. Still, we recommend users looking for a more interactive experience to move over to Discord.

Officially moving to Discord has been something we’ve been looking into doing for quite some time now, and with the marketplace looming and the potential uptick in new users it may bring, it only seems logical to make this move now. We are extremely excited by the new possibilities having our entire community gathered within the same platform opens up, and we fully intend on making the best out of this situation to enhance your community experience.
So come to
join us today on Discord
and let the fun begin!
The Particl team

node.plaintext:
🎉 It’s official! 🎉

Earlier today, the Particl network successfully went through our planned mainnet
hardfork without any issue — at block 397010
[https://explorer.particl.io/block/a40ab1e697f8833aa961d59e84ca839b443cc4bdfc80fd1aa8ba784559ce9e28]
.

Insight
[https://explorer.particl.io/block/a40ab1e697f8833aa961d59e84ca839b443cc4bdfc80fd1aa8ba784559ce9e28]
Particl Insight. View detailed information on all particl transactions and
blocks.
[https://explorer.particl.io/block/a40ab1e697f8833aa961d59e84ca839b443cc4bdfc80fd1aa8ba784559ce9e28]
explorer.particl.io
[https://explorer.particl.io/block/a40ab1e697f8833aa961d59e84ca839b443cc4bdfc80fd1aa8ba784559ce9e28]

As a reminder, the main driver for the planned hardfork is to onboard features
needed for our upcoming marketplace to be on the main network chain. Particl
added both multi-signature cold staking capabilities and paid SMSG. To see what
these two features mean, head over to our explanatory blog post linked just
below to know all the details.

Development Update - Planned Hardfork Schedule - Particl News
[https://particl.news/development-update-planned-hardfork-schedule-5051238feac0]
2019.02.16-12:00:00 UTC In 4 days, on the 16th of February, we're going to fork
Particl's test network (testnet) to…
[https://particl.news/development-update-planned-hardfork-schedule-5051238feac0]
particl.news
[https://particl.news/development-update-planned-hardfork-schedule-5051238feac0]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Next steps
The success of this feature onboarding means the Particl network now has the
tools ready and available on mainnet for our beta version of the private,
decentralized, and community-governed marketplace. 😎

It is important to keep in mind, while multi-signature cold staking is not
directly related to the marketplace, paid SMSG allows for the decentralized
governance mechanism to properly function with real coins.

It is also important to remember that the success of the hardfork doesn’t mean
the highly anticipated marketplace is ready today.

The team is still making the final preparations, testing, and fixes leading up
to the beta mainnet release. Head over to our #testing
[https://discord.gg/YFeXbFZ] channel on Discord to be able to download the
latest testnet version of the marketplace and see where it currently stands. The
great news is — with the help of all the community testers — the testnet version
of alpha/beta marketplace is now pretty stable. We only have a few fixes left to
implement before we are ready to release the first ever private-by-default
decentralized marketplace onto mainnet.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

With Beta mainnet just around the corner, if you are considering selling or
buying stuff for crypto, now is probably a good time to reach out to any vendor
you had in mind to sell on the marketplace. All we can say is buckle up because
we’re just about to show crypto what a blockchain product should look and feel
like!


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

node.plaintext:
As many of you know, the PART mainnet network is going to undergo a hardfork on
the 1st of March 2019
[https://particl.news/development-update-planned-hardfork-schedule-5051238feac0]
. This hardfork will port two testnet features onto mainnet: multi-signature
cold staking and paid SMSG. Because hardforks are a pretty big deal, it is
important for you to prepare your nodes in prevision of this event so that you
are not left staking on a deprecated chain. Luckily, the whole preparation
process is extremely easy.

Development Update - Planned Hardfork Schedule - Particl News
[https://particl.news/development-update-planned-hardfork-schedule-5051238feac0]
2019.02.16-12:00:00 UTC In 4 days, on the 16th of February, we're going to fork
Particl's test network (testnet) to…
[https://particl.news/development-update-planned-hardfork-schedule-5051238feac0]
particl.news
[https://particl.news/development-update-planned-hardfork-schedule-5051238feac0]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

How to Prepare
The only thing you need to make sure is that the nodes you are running are
updated to Particl Core 0.17.1.3, or, at the very least, Particl Core 0.17.1.2. 
Any version prior to this one will not be compatible with the hardforked chain
and will therefore make you stake on the wrong chain. This will make all of your
staking rewards invalid and any transaction made on that wrong chain will not be
reflected on the hardforked chain. In other words, you absolutely need to update
your node before the hardfork if you wish to use PART post-hardfork.

Here’s how to do it, depending on the client you use.

Particl Desktop | Particl Qt/cli | Partyman | Cold Staking Pool | Particl Copay 
| On Exchange

I. Particl Desktop
If you’re using Particl Desktop, then lucky you, because it is the easiest of
all clients to update. Running Particl Desktop 1.2.x and beyond means your
wallet will automatically notify you as soon as there’s a new Particl Core
version available. All you have to do after getting this notification is close
your Particl Desktop wallet and reopen it. Your Particl Core daemon should
automatically update upon reopening the client.

As a precautionary measure, even if you are running Particl Desktop 1.2.x and
beyond, it is recommended to manually check if the wallet has properly updated.
There shouldn’t be any issue, but you can never be too careful when a hardforks
coming.

.Additionally, if you are running a Particl Desktop version older than 1.3.1,
then we highly recommend, as always, upgrading to the latest version
[https://particl.news/particl-desktop-1-3-1-released-8802e7b326fb] as it fixes
various issues, bugs, and contains many improvements.

Download Particl Desktop 1.3.1: 
https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1

Particl Desktop 1.3.1 Announcement & Changelog: 
https://particl.news/particl-desktop-1-3-1-released-8802e7b326fb

particl/particl-desktop
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1]
Particl Angular GUI - The source for the Particl GUI. - particl/particl-desktop
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1]github.com
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1]

II. Particl-Qt & Particl-cli
If you’re running either Particl-Qt or Particl-cli, then you will need to
manually download and install the latest version of Particl Core. Simply visit
the Particl-Core Github release page
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/] and download the latest
version.

Note: The latest Particl Core version is 0.17.1.3, not 0.17.1.2. However, as
long as you run a Particl Core version that is equal or more recent than
0.17.1.2, you will have no issue with the hardfork. Particl Core 0.17.1.2 is the
MINIMUM version required for your to follow along with the network’s hardfork.

Download Particl Core 0.17.1.3: 
https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.17.1.3

particl/particl-core
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.17.1.3]
Particl Core integration/staging tree. Contribute to particl/particl-core
development by creating an account on GitHub.
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.17.1.3]github.com
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.17.1.3]

III. Partyman (Cold Staking App)
If you are using Partyman to cold stake your funds, then whenever you enter a
command within the app, it should automatically prompt you to install the newest
version. In order to properly update your node, you will first need to upgrade
your Partyman app, then update your Core. In order to do this, follow the steps
below:

 * Go into your Partyman folder (cd /root/partyman)
 * Type the command git pull. This will update the Partyman app.
 * Once done, update Particl Core by typing./partyman update
 * Accept the update by typing y
 * Once updated, restart your Particl Core by typing./partyman restart
 * Accept the restart by typing y

IV. Cold Staking Pool
If you are cold staking from a pool, you don’t need to do anything other than
update whatever wallet you use to access your funds. Usually, this would be
Particl-Qt or Particl Desktop. Refer to the instructions above to properly
update.

If you are a pool operator, you will need to update both the Particl Core and
the packages of your pool. Follow these steps:

 * First update your pool’s packages: 
   https://github.com/particl/coldstakepool/blob/master/doc/upgrade_old_install_to_package.txt
 * Then, update your Particl Core: 
   https://github.com/particl/coldstakepool/blob/master/doc/upgrade_daemon.txt

V. Particl Copay Wallet
If you are using Particl’s mobile application, Particl Copay, then you don’t
need to do anything. The nodes Particl Copay connects to will automatically
update in time for the hardfork.

VI. Coins on Exchange
If your coins are stored on an exchange, then you don’t need to do anything. The
exchange will update its nodes in time for the hardfork. Exchanges and other
third-parties running Particl nodes have already been made aware of the upcoming
hardfork.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By following these instructions, you should be equipped to navigate any standard
issue during or after the hardfork. If you have any question, don’t hesitate to 
join our Discord [https://discord.gg/YFeXbFZ] server and ask your questions in
the #support channel. We’ll be happy to help you out!

In the meantime, we can’t wait to go through this hardfork and add to Particl’s
network these two awesome features!

The Particl Team

Running Particl Desktop 1.2.x and beyond means your wallet will automatically notify you as soon as there’s a new Particl Core version available. All you have to do after getting this notification is close your Particl Desktop wallet and reopen it. Your Particl Core daemon should automatically update upon reopening the client.

As a precautionary measure, even if you are running Particl Desktop 1.2.x and beyond, it is recommended to manually check if the wallet has properly updated. There shouldn’t be any issue, but you can never be too careful when a hardforks coming.

Additionally, if you are running a Particl Desktop version older than 1.3.1, then we highly recommend, as always, upgrading to the latest version as it fixes various issues, bugs, and contains many improvements.

Download Particl Desktop 1.3.1: https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1

Particl Desktop 1.3.1 Announcement & Changelog: https://particl.news/particl-desktop-1-3-1-released-8802e7b326fb

particl/particl-desktop
Particl Angular GUI - The source for the Particl GUI. - particl/particl-desktopgithub.com

II. Particl-Qt & Particl-cli

If you’re running either Particl-Qt or Particl-cli, then you will need to manually download and install the latest version of Particl Core. Simply visit the Particl-Core Github release page and download the latest version.

Note: The latest Particl Core version is 0.17.1.3, not 0.17.1.2. However, as long as you run a Particl Core version that is equal or more recent than 0.17.1.2, you will have no issue with the hardfork. Particl Core 0.17.1.2 is the MINIMUM version required for your to follow along with the network’s hardfork.

Download Particl Core 0.17.1.3: https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.17.1.3

particl/particl-core
Particl Core integration/staging tree. Contribute to particl/particl-core development by creating an account on GitHub.github.com

III. Partyman (Cold Staking App)

If you are using Partyman to cold stake your funds, then whenever you enter a command within the app, it should automatically prompt you to install the newest version. In order to properly update your node, you will first need to upgrade your Partyman app, then update your Core. In order to do this, follow the steps below:

  • Go into your Partyman folder (cd /root/partyman)
  • Type the command git pull. This will update the Partyman app.
  • Once done, update Particl Core by typing ./partyman update
  • Accept the update by typing y
  • Once updated, restart your Particl Core by typing ./partyman restart
  • Accept the restart by typing y

IV. Cold Staking Pool

If you are cold staking from a pool, you don’t need to do anything other than update whatever wallet you use to access your funds. Usually, this would be Particl-Qt or Particl Desktop. Refer to the instructions above to properly update.

If you are a pool operator, you will need to update both the Particl Core and the packages of your pool. Follow these steps:

  • First update your pool’s packages: https://github.com/particl/coldstakepool/blob/master/doc/upgrade_old_install_to_package.txt
  • Then, update your Particl Core: https://github.com/particl/coldstakepool/blob/master/doc/upgrade_daemon.txt

V. Particl Copay Wallet

If you are using Particl’s mobile application, Particl Copay, then you don’t need to do anything. The nodes Particl Copay connects to will automatically update in time for the hardfork.

VI. Coins on Exchange

If your coins are stored on an exchange, then you don’t need to do anything. The exchange will update its nodes in time for the hardfork. Exchanges and other third-parties running Particl nodes have already been made aware of the upcoming hardfork.

By following these instructions, you should be equipped to navigate any standard issue during or after the hardfork. If you have any question, don’t hesitate to join our Discord server and ask your questions in the #support channel. We’ll be happy to help you out!

In the meantime, we can’t wait to go through this hardfork and add to Particl’s network these two awesome features!

The Particl Team

Particl Desktop 1.3.1 Receive PageEarlier today, we released Particl Desktop
1.3.1 which had been in its pre-release stage for a bit more than two weeks now.
This new Particl Desktop version coincides with the update of Particl Core to
its 0.17.1.2 which is the minimum Core version you need to have installed in
order to properly go through the coming hardfork on the 1st of March. This
update fixes both the ZAP function to activate cold staking directly from your
Particl Desktop wallet and various minor issues that were identified during this
version’s pre-release stage.

Note: Particl Desktop version 1.3.0 will also automatically update its Particl
Core version next time you open the wallet, so upgrading Desktop to 1.3.1 is not
a pre-requisite for the hardfork although it is, as always, recommended to
update to the latest version.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Download Particl Desktop v.1.3.1: 
https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1

particl/particl-desktop
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1]
Particl Angular GUI - The source for the Particl GUI. - particl/particl-desktop
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1]github.com
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases/tag/1.3.1]

Changelog
 * Fix to the ZAP function (will now work when connecting to a cold staking
   pool)
 * Various minor issues
 * Automatically updates to Particl Core 0.17.1.2 (works for the previous
   Particl Desktop 1.3.0 version as well)


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

⚠️ IMPORTANT ⚠️
As the Particl network is going to go through a hardfork on the 1st of March, it
is imperative that you update to Particl Core 0.17.1.2. While Particl Desktop
version 1.2.x and beyond should update your core automatically, we highly
recommend you manually verify the update has successfully been executed.

Additionally, if you are running a cold staking node, a cold staking pool, or
Particl-Qt/Particl-cli, then you will need to manually update your Core to at
least version 0.17.1.2 by following this link (following this link will lead you
to download version 0.17.1.3 which is the latest): 
https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.17.1.3

node.plaintext:
At the end of last year, we said we wanted to get Particl at more events around
the world to increase the project’s booths-on-the-ground exposure. It started
with a demo day in Toronto, followed by an evening of Particl-related 
presentations at
[https://particl.news/particl-in-paris-presentations-at-coinhouse-a38106cdd36a]
CoinHouse
[https://particl.news/particl-in-paris-presentations-at-coinhouse-a38106cdd36a] 
in Paris
[https://particl.news/particl-in-paris-presentations-at-coinhouse-a38106cdd36a],
and then by Asia Crypto Week’s flagship event in Hong Kong, TOKEN2049
[https://particl.news/particl-is-going-to-hong-kongs-token-2049-conference-e1fca540740b]
. Still with the same intentions in mind, Particl is now headed to the 2019
edition of the mega Consensus conference in New-York City from the 13th of May
to the 15th.

Widely known and popular within the blockchain industry, Consensus is the
biggest crypto event of the year, bringing together all the biggest and most
innovative projects of the space as well as its top advocates. This will be the
ideal occasion to meet as many of you as possible and get Particl, as well as
the Open Marketplace more specifically, in front of the key people in the
industry.

To make sure we get as much exposure as we can, we’ll also have our very own
booth on the same floor as popular projects and companies such as Ledger and
Rootstock, only to name a few. We’ll be setting up a marketplace station where
anyone will be able to test out the Open Marketplace in real-time, on mainnet,
and will have a couple of Particl team members present at the event to present
the project as well as get some networking going.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Consensus 2019 - CoinDesk [https://www.coindesk.com/events/consensus-2019]
Consensus is the annual gathering of the cryptocurrency and blockchain
technology world. Since 2015, Consensus has…
[https://www.coindesk.com/events/consensus-2019]www.coindesk.com
[https://www.coindesk.com/events/consensus-2019]

> Consensus is the annual gathering of the cryptocurrency and blockchain
technology world. Since 2015, Consensus has attracted every major company,
developer, founder and investor in the cryptocurrency and blockchain world to
engage in an annual discussion about the future of the industry.Consensus is
designed to be a big tent for the industry: it convenes industry participants
ranging from enterprise consortia to cypher punks. This convening power breaks
down the barriers that separate companies in the industry and allows everyone
working on the technology to spend three days learning from their peers.
Previous speakers reflect the richness and diversity of the Consensus crowd,
ranging from titans of Wall Street and rulers of the Fortune 500, to pioneering
cryptographers and the core developers who power the world’s most valuable
cryptocurrencies. —Consensus 2019
[https://www.coindesk.com/events/consensus-2019]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get Your Ticket Discount
The reason we’re going to Consensus 2019 is not only to present the project in
front of the biggest crypto crowd. We want to get to meet you as well! As such,
we’re making available our promo code for the event which will save you a
whopping $300 on your ticket when making the purchase. To get the code is easy,
simply follow these steps:

 * Create a Discord account if you don’t have one already
 * Join our Discord server by following this link: https://discord.gg/YFeXbFZ
 * If your account is a new one, accept the rules and the terms & conditions of
   the server
 * In the #general channel, type!consensus2019
 * Check your DMs, a bot will send you a direct message with your promo code in
   it

We hope to see you there and chat a bit with you! Consensus 2019 should be an
exciting event and we are thrilled to get the Open Marketplace at the very
forefront of the cryptocurrency scene and show the world what we’ve been up to
since the launch of the Particl project!

node.plaintext:
Preparing Mainnet for a Private, Decentralized Marketplace
Over the past few weeks we’ve started preparing the groundwork for Particl’s
Open Marketplace first mainnet release. During this time, the team and community
testers have been hard at work identifying & fixing the last of the small issues
still present in the latest testnet builds.

With a stable build nearing completion, it is imperative to prepare our main
Particl Network blockchain for the addition of a privacy-focused decentralized
marketplace. The first step is a code update that will allow decentralized
governance to work properly (with coin weight) and requires hardfork. Ahead of
this, and a non-event for most users, will be a hardfork of our Particl test
network as well.

Here are the things you need to know.

Planned Hardfork Schedule
Testnet Fork (Particl Core 0.18)
⭐️ 2019.02.16 —12:00:00 UTC

 * VARIABLE SMSG FEE
 * MULTISIG COLDSTAKING
 * BULLETPROOFS

Mainnet Fork (Particl Core 0.17)
⭐️ 2019.03.01 —12:00:00 UTC

 * PAID SMSG
 * MULTISIG COLDSTAKING


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Testnet Hardfork (16th of February)
> 2019.02.16–12:00:00 UTC
In 4 days, on the 16th of February, we’re going to fork Particl’s test network
(testnet) to merge a couple of features that have been sitting in RegTest
(internal pre-testnet network) for some time now. This hardfork doesn’t require
you to do anything but, of course, it will allow you to test these new features
on our public testnet.

Bulletproofs
This testnet hardfork is going to include Bulletproofs, the Confidential
Transactions protocol improvement that will reduce the size of CT/RingCT
transactions by ~70%. Bulletproofs is an important brick in Particl’s wall of
privacy as it will allow RingCT and CT transactions to be executed without
bloating the blockchain and bringing its size to absurd levels. We believe
privacy and scalability can and should go hand in hand.

Why make compromises when you can have the best of both worlds?

Multi-Signature Cold Staking
As some of you already know, we are already working on a major new feature to
our mobile app, Particl Copay; Cold staking capabilities! This testnet hardfork
will add multi-signature cold staking support, meaning it will now be possible
to cold stake funds held collectively by multiple devices.

This will give you access to the utmost level of privacy Proof-of-Stake can
offer.

In fact, cold staking from multi-signature addresses means you will need to have
access to more than one device to move funds around. This constitutes a solid
barrier to anyone looking into stealing your coins as they would be required to
get a hold of all of your signing devices.

> Note: Ledger Nano devices won’t initially be compatible with multi-signature
cold staking, however we’ve already identified that, and with the help of a
custom script, it could be possible to make the hardware devices compatible.
This may not come immediately, but is good to know for future endeavors!
Variable SMSG Fee
This feature is probably not an exciting one at first sight but will be very
important when it comes to implementing advanced escrow features. This protocol
improvement allows SMSG messages to have various fees, allowing, for example,
custom escrow security deposit ratios (agree with the other party to put any %
of the item’s value as security deposit instead of 100% every time). You won’t
really be able to test variable SMSG in a practical way until this feature is
integrated into the escrow system. But, it’s nonetheless an important feature
making its way onto testnet.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mainnet Hardfork
> 2019.03.01–12:00:00 UTC
In order for the Open Marketplace to go live a couple things need to happen; the
trustless escrow system needs to be adapted to our OMP-library for blinded
escrow (privacy), and Paid SMSG needs to be onboarded to mainnet. The latter
requires a planned hardfork which will happen on March 1st 2019, at 12:00 am GMT
.

Hardforking is a pretty big deal, especially as it requires the greater majority
of nodes to update in a timely manner. This requirement also applies to
third-parties and exchanges to ensure that they do not run an outdated fork of
the project. For this reason, we’ve already started contacting third-parties
last week to let them know the improvements coming to the Particl Network and be
leaders in the shift to the new chain.

> Note: for users of Particl Desktop and Particl Copay mobile apps, the clients
will automatically upgrade and sync with the correct chain seamlessly in the
background. For most users both planned hardforks will be non-events but we
wanted to responsibly let you know what is coming on March 1st.
Paid SMSG Messages
This feature is necessary in order for the governance system to function
properly on mainnet. This is the missing piece that needs to be added onto
mainnet (it already works flawlessly on testnet) so that governance votes (both
proposals and listing flags) can be properly registered with coin weight.

We cannot release the marketplace on mainnet without this critical feature.

Multi-Signature Cold Staking
As mentioned above, we’ll be hardforking multi-signature cold staking
capabilities onto testnet on the 16th of February. This will give 2 weeks for
testing properly this protocol enhancement after which we’ll merge onto mainnet
at the same time as the paid SMSG message hardfork. This should reduce the risks
associated with executing hardforks by combining both these items into the same
hardfork.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We’d also like to thank everybody that has helped us test the latest testnet
version of the marketplace. We are still working on preparing the groundwork for
the Open Marketplace’s mainnet launch, and wanted to relay parts of the critical
path in an easy, step-by-step manner so everyone on the same page.

In the meantime, you can relax and watch our latest AMA/FAQ Session
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eoB1_rNWvEM], Paul’s latest interview with
CryptoTradingFR (part 1 [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lN1bEatbNrQ], part 2
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DI-lG14WktE&t=1s]), or just launch and play
with the latest testnet builds
[https://particl.news/new-open-marketplace-testnet-builds-a45fbc699fc5] of
Particl’s Open Marketplace!


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Preparing Mainnet for a Private, Decentralized Marketplace

Over the past few weeks we’ve started preparing the groundwork for Particl’s Open Marketplace first mainnet release. During this time, the team and community testers have been hard at work identifying & fixing the last of the small issues still present in the latest testnet builds.

With a stable build nearing completion, it is imperative to prepare our main Particl Network blockchain for the addition of a privacy-focused decentralized marketplace. The first step is a code update that will allow decentralized governance to work properly (with coin weight) and requires hardfork. Ahead of this, and a non-event for most users, will be a hardfork of our Particl test network as well.

Here are the things you need to know.

Planned Hardfork Schedule

Testnet Fork (Particl Core 0.18)

⭐️ 2019.02.16 — 12:00:00 UTC

  • VARIABLE SMSG FEE
  • MULTISIG COLDSTAKING
  • BULLETPROOFS

Mainnet Fork (Particl Core 0.17)

⭐️ 2019.03.01 — 12:00:00 UTC

  • PAID SMSG
  • MULTISIG COLDSTAKING

Testnet Hardfork (16th of February)

2019.02.16–12:00:00 UTC

In 4 days, on the 16th of February, we’re going to fork Particl’s test network (testnet) to merge a couple of features that have been sitting in RegTest (internal pre-testnet network) for some time now. This hardfork doesn’t require you to do anything but, of course, it will allow you to test these new features on our public testnet.

Bulletproofs

This testnet hardfork is going to include Bulletproofs, the Confidential Transactions protocol improvement that will reduce the size of CT/RingCT transactions by ~70%. Bulletproofs is an important brick in Particl’s wall of privacy as it will allow RingCT and CT transactions to be executed without bloating the blockchain and bringing its size to absurd levels. We believe privacy and scalability can and should go hand in hand.

Why make compromises when you can have the best of both worlds?

Multi-Signature Cold Staking

As some of you already know, we are already working on a major new feature to our mobile app, Particl Copay; Cold staking capabilities! This testnet hardfork will add multi-signature cold staking support, meaning it will now be possible to cold stake funds held collectively by multiple devices.

This will give you access to the utmost level of privacy Proof-of-Stake can offer.

In fact, cold staking from multi-signature addresses means you will need to have access to more than one device to move funds around. This constitutes a solid barrier to anyone looking into stealing your coins as they would be required to get a hold of all of your signing devices.

Note: Ledger Nano devices won’t initially be compatible with multi-signature cold staking, however we’ve already identified that, and with the help of a custom script, it could be possible to make the hardware devices compatible. This may not come immediately, but is good to know for future endeavors!

Variable SMSG Fee

This feature is probably not an exciting one at first sight but will be very important when it comes to implementing advanced escrow features. This protocol improvement allows SMSG messages to have various fees, allowing, for example, custom escrow security deposit ratios (agree with the other party to put any % of the item’s value as security deposit instead of 100% every time). You won’t really be able to test variable SMSG in a practical way until this feature is integrated into the escrow system. But, it’s nonetheless an important feature making its way onto testnet.

Mainnet Hardfork

2019.03.01–12:00:00 UTC

In order for the Open Marketplace to go live a couple things need to happen; the trustless escrow system needs to be adapted to our OMP-library for blinded escrow (privacy), and Paid SMSG needs to be onboarded to mainnet. The latter requires a planned hardfork which will happen on March 1st 2019, at 12:00 am GMT.

Hardforking is a pretty big deal, especially as it requires the greater majority of nodes to update in a timely manner. This requirement also applies to third-parties and exchanges to ensure that they do not run an outdated fork of the project. For this reason, we’ve already started contacting third-parties last week to let them know the improvements coming to the Particl Network and be leaders in the shift to the new chain.

Note: for users of Particl Desktop and Particl Copay mobile apps, the clients will automatically upgrade and sync with the correct chain seamlessly in the background. For most users both planned hardforks will be non-events but we wanted to responsibly let you know what is coming on March 1st.

Paid SMSG Messages

This feature is necessary in order for the governance system to function properly on mainnet. This is the missing piece that needs to be added onto mainnet (it already works flawlessly on testnet) so that governance votes (both proposals and listing flags) can be properly registered with coin weight.

We cannot release the marketplace on mainnet without this critical feature.

Multi-Signature Cold Staking

As mentioned above, we’ll be hardforking multi-signature cold staking capabilities onto testnet on the 16th of February. This will give 2 weeks for testing properly this protocol enhancement after which we’ll merge onto mainnet at the same time as the paid SMSG message hardfork. This should reduce the risks associated with executing hardforks by combining both these items into the same hardfork.

We’d also like to thank everybody that has helped us test the latest testnet version of the marketplace. We are still working on preparing the groundwork for the Open Marketplace’s mainnet launch, and wanted to relay parts of the critical path in an easy, step-by-step manner so everyone on the same page.

In the meantime, you can relax and watch our latest AMA/FAQ Session, Paul’s latest interview with CryptoTradingFR (part 1, part 2), or just launch and play with the latest testnet builds of Particl’s Open Marketplace!

node.html:
Preparing Mainnet for a Private, Decentralized Marketplace

Over the past few weeks, Particl has started preparing the groundwork for Particl’s Open Marketplace first mainnet release. During this time, the team and community testers have been hard at work identifying & fixing the last of the small issues still present in the latest testnet builds.

With a stable build nearing completion, it is imperative that Particl’s main network is prepared for the addition of a privacy-focused decentralized marketplace. The first step is a code update that will allow decentralized governance to work properly (with coin weight). Ahead of this, and a non-event for most users, will be a hardfork of our Particl test network as well.

Here are the things you need to know.

Planned Hardfork Schedule

Testnet Fork (Particl Core 0.18)

⭐️ 2019.02.16–12:00:00

  • VARIABLE SMSG FEE
  • MULTISIG COLDSTAKING
  • BULLETPROOFS

Mainnet Fork (Particl Core 0.17)

⭐️ 2019.03.01–12:00:00

  • PAID SMSG
  • MULTISIG COLDSTAKING

Testnet Hardfork (16th of February)

2019.02.16–12:00:00

In 4 days, on the 16th of February, Particl’s test network (testnet) is going to fork in order to merge a couple of features that have been sitting in RegTest (internal pre-testnet network) for some time now. If you are a Particl holder, this hardfork doesn’t require you to do anything but, of course, it will allow you to test these new features on our public testnet.

Bulletproofs

This testnet hardfork is going to include Bulletproofs, the Confidential Transactions protocol improvement that will reduce the size of CT/RingCT transactions by ~70%. Bulletproofs is an important brick in Particl’s wall of privacy as it will allow RingCT and CT transactions to be executed without bloating the blockchain and bringing its size to absurd levels. Privacy and scalability can and should go hand in hand.

Why make compromises when you can have the best of both worlds?

Multi-Signature Cold Staking

Particl has been working on a major new feature to its mobile app, Particl Copay; Cold staking capabilities! This testnet hardfork will add multi-signature cold staking support, meaning it will now be possible to cold stake funds held collectively by multiple devices.

This will give users access to the utmost level of privacy Proof-of-Stake can offer.

In fact, cold staking from multi-signature addresses means users will need to have access to more than one device to move funds around. This constitutes a solid barrier to anyone looking into stealing coins as they would be required to get a hold of all of the signing devices.

Note: Ledger Nano devices won’t initially be compatible with multi-signature cold staking, however, Particl has already identified that, and with the help of a custom script, it could be possible to make the hardware devices compatible. This may not come immediately, but is good to know for future endeavors!

Variable SMSG Fee

This feature is probably not an exciting one at first sight but will be very important when it comes to implementing advanced escrow features. This protocol improvement allows SMSG messages to have various fees, allowing, for example, custom escrow security deposit ratios (agree with the other party to put any % of the item’s value as security deposit instead of 100% every time). It won’t really be possible to test variable SMSG in a practical way until this feature is integrated into the escrow system. But, it’s nonetheless an important feature making its way onto testnet.

Mainnet Hardfork

2019.03.01–12:00:00

In order for the Open Marketplace to go live a couple things need to happen; the trustless escrow system needs to be adapted to our OMP-library for blinded escrow (privacy), and Paid SMSG needs to be onboarded to mainnet. The latter requires a planned hardfork which will happen on March 1st 2019, at 12:00 am GMT.

Hardforking is a pretty big deal, especially as it requires the greater majority of nodes to update in a timely manner. This requirement also applies to third-parties and exchanges to ensure that they do not run an outdated fork of the project. For this reason, Particl has already started contacting third-parties last week to let them know the improvements coming to the Particl Network and be leaders in the shift to the new chain.

Note: for users of Particl Desktop and Particl Copay mobile apps, the clients will automatically upgrade and sync with the correct chain seamlessly in the background. For most users both planned hardforks will be non-events but we wanted to responsibly let you know what is coming on March 1st.

Paid SMSG Messages

This feature is necessary in order for the governance system to function properly on mainnet. This is the missing piece that needs to be added onto mainnet (it already works flawlessly on testnet) so that governance votes (both proposals and listing flags) can be properly registered with coin weight.

The marketplace cannot release on mainnet without this critical feature.

Multi-Signature Cold Staking

As mentioned above, Particl will be hardforking multi-signature cold staking capabilities onto testnet on the 16th of February. This will give 2 weeks for testing properly this protocol enhancement after which it’ll merge onto mainnet at the same time as the paid SMSG message hardfork. This should reduce the risks associated with executing hardforks by combining both these items into the same hardfork.

The Particl team would also like to thank everybody that has helped them test the latest testnet version of the marketplace. We are still working on preparing the groundwork for the Open Marketplace’s mainnet launch, and wanted to relay parts of the critical path in an easy, step-by-step manner so everyone on the same page.

In the meantime, you can relax and watch our latest AMA/FAQ Session, Paul’s latest interview with CryptoTradingFR (part 1, part 2), or just launch and play with the latest testnet builds of Particl’s Open Marketplace!

As Stable as Ever
For the last few weeks, the Particl team has been pouring all of its heart into
making the current testnet build of the Open Marketplace as stable and bug-free
as possible in prevision of its move onto mainnet.

Earlier yesterday, we released new and very stable testnet builds
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/pull/1407]. These builds contain all
of the features and functions the marketplace will launch with on mainnet. They
are also is in a very stable state, definitely hinting at just how close we are
from a merge onto mainnet.

Latest Testnet Build
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get Started!
Download and get started with testing the Open Marketplace by following this
Github link:

Community Test Build 20190205 by zaSmilingIdiot · Pull Request #1407 ·
particl/particl-desktop [https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/pull/1407]
GitHub is home to over 28 million developers working together to host and
review
code, manage projects, and build…
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/pull/1407]github.com
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/pull/1407]

Testnet Faucet (get free testnet coins): http://faucet.particl.xyz/

We also recommend logging any issue you may encounter on the Issue section
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/issues] of our Github page.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Important Note
For those of you that have tested the marketplace on testnet before, please do
remember that you will need to wipe your existing marketplace database,
otherwise, you will encounter errors. This will be sorted in the future, but is
highly recommended for the moment. The location of your particl-market is
dependent on the OS you use.

Windows:C:\Users\<User>\AppData\Roaming\particl-market

macOS:~/Library/Application Support/particl-market

Linux:~/.particl-market


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Changelog
 * Coin Weight
 * Bug fixes
 * Stability improvements
 * UI/UX improvements


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Join the rest of the community and help us find any small issue left standing
before mainnet!

We are thrilled to announce that Particl will be holding an event directly at
the headquarters of CoinHouse, France’s most famous crypto hub, on the 21st of
February 2019 from 7:00PM to 8:30PM CET.

Particl : Le commerce en ligne sans intermédiaire
[https://www.meetup.com/fr-FR/LMDB-CH/events/258656626/]
Particl est une plateforme hybride blockchain/P2P ainsi qu'une crypto-monnaie
anonyme en Proof-of-Stake créée et mise à…
[https://www.meetup.com/fr-FR/LMDB-CH/events/258656626/]www.meetup.com
[https://www.meetup.com/fr-FR/LMDB-CH/events/258656626/]

CoinHouse is the leading Bitcoin and cryptocurrency hub in France
[https://business.lesechos.fr/entrepreneurs/idees-de-business/0600016133318-coinhouse-le-grand-magasin-du-bitcoin-324430.php] 
with over 100,000 customers, 35,000 of them being considered as “active”. It
offers a wide array of services including educational information, investment
advice
[https://www.capital.fr/entreprises-marches/bitcoin-coinhouse-peut-desormais-faire-du-conseil-en-investissement-1305686] 
 (approved by France’s AMF), OTC Bitcoin purchases and sales, and more. As part
of their educational mission and community-driven initiatives, they hold very
popular meetups, conferences, and other types of events that can be attended by
the France cryptocurrency community. The startup has also been the home of the
now widely-known and popular startup Ledger [http://ledger.com], which one of
their product (Ledger Nano S as well as the upcoming Ledger Nano X) is 
compatible with Particl for hardware storage and cold staking
[https://particl.news/particl-introduces-hardware-cold-staking-daa093d124ae].
CoinHouse is also partnered with renowned Paris Universities HEC
[http://www.hec.edu/] and Polytechnique [https://www.polytechnique.edu/] and
offers blockchain classes to students.

La Maison du Bitcoin Rebrands as Coinhouse

node.plaintext:
We are very excited to announce that Particl Copay, Particl’s mobile wallet, is
now available on iOS! You can now download Particl Copay on the App Store and
get started with Particl on your iPhone today.

‎Particl Copay
[https://itunes.apple.com/nl/app/particl-copay/id1449589645?l=en&mt=8]
‎The Copay wallet has a convenient payment proposal flow that supports
multi-signature addresses and batch processing…
[https://itunes.apple.com/nl/app/particl-copay/id1449589645?l=en&mt=8]
itunes.apple.com
[https://itunes.apple.com/nl/app/particl-copay/id1449589645?l=en&mt=8]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

With this release, iPhone users will now be able to enjoy all of the great
features that have been available on the Android and Desktop versions of Particl
Copay:

 * Multiple wallet creation and management in-app
 * Multi-Currency Support
 * Intuitive, multisignature security for personal or shared wallets
 * Easy spending proposal flow for shared wallets and group payments
 * BIP32 Hierarchical deterministic (HD) address generation and wallet backups
 * Device-based security: all private keys are stored locally, not in the cloud
 * Synchronous access across all major mobile and desktop platforms
 * Payment protocol (BIP70-BIP73) support: easily-identifiable payment requests
   and verifiable, secure Particl and Bitcoin payments
 * Mnemonic (BIP39) support for wallet backups
 * Paper wallet sweep support (BIP38)
 * Multiple languages supported

What’s more, it can also be expected that the iOS version of Particl Copay will
also get all of the improvements and upgrades mentioned in our latest Particl
Copay Development Update
[https://particl.news/particl-copay-mobile-app-update-6bc8cd04f48] such as
mobile cold staking and multi-signature cold staking when these features are
ready to go live.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

‎Particl Copay
‎The Copay wallet has a convenient payment proposal flow that supports multi-signature addresses and batch processing…itunes.apple.com

  • Intuitive, multisignature security for personal or shared wallets
  • Easy spending proposal flow for shared wallets and group payments
  • BIP32 Hierarchical deterministic (HD) address generation and wallet backups
  • Device-based security: all private keys are stored locally, not in the cloud
  • Synchronous access across all major mobile and desktop platforms
  • Payment protocol (BIP70-BIP73) support: easily-identifiable payment requests and verifiable, secure Particl and Bitcoin payments
  • Mnemonic (BIP39) support for wallet backups
  • Paper wallet sweep support (BIP38)

What’s more, it can also be expected that the iOS version of Particl Copay will also get all of the improvements and upgrades mentioned in our latest Particl Copay Development Update such as mobile cold staking and multi-signature cold staking when these features are ready to go live.

node.plaintext:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Want to read the full version of this blog post? Then simply follow this link to
the original version:

New Release Schedule for Particl's Open Marketplace
[https://particl.news/new-release-schedule-for-particls-open-marketplace-af43c2851074]
The Timeline As stated in an answer to a question asked during our latest
AMA/FAQ session, one of the decisions the…
[https://particl.news/new-release-schedule-for-particls-open-marketplace-af43c2851074]
particl.news
[https://particl.news/new-release-schedule-for-particls-open-marketplace-af43c2851074]


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Particl’s Open Marketplace Alpha Undergoing its Final Tweaks
The Particl team is making the final touches to make the current testnet alpha
build completely stable and, hopefully, bug-free. No new features is currently
being implemented until the Alpha build is made entirely stable. Having a super
stable build will make integrating new features on it much faster and easier and
should lead to more regular (and exciting) releases.

The Alpha Version Moves Up to Mainnet
Once the testnet Alpha version of the marketplace is fully stable and thoroughly
tested, it will be moved up onto Particl’s mainnet, allowing you to purchase and
sell real items using real PART coins. This milestone event will mark the end of
the Alpha phase of development and initiate the Beta phase.

Limited Features
This first ever mainnet version of Particl’s Open Marketplace won’t require any
sort of hardfork and will only contain a limited set of features. The features
available will be the same features that have been on testnet for some time.
This includes decentralized governance with coin weight, as well as the complete
buy and sell flow. Community proposals, although not directly a marketplace
feature, will also be live on mainnet.

No Privacy Enhancing Features in This Version
It’s very important to note that this mainnet version won’t be coupled with the
RingCT hardfork yet, meaning that the MAD escrow system won’t be enhanced with
it. This means that all marketplace transactions made on this version of the
marketplace will be pseudonymous, not anonymous, until RingCT is hardforked onto
mainnet and integrated into the escrow system during Beta. You can rest assured,
however, that no other data will be generated by the marketplace.

The Beta Phase Will be Focused on Adding New Features
New features will be introduced during the Beta phase and will be integrated
into existing testing builds as Release Candidates
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_release_life_cycle#Release_candidate].
Release Candidates will be released on testnet, and mainnet implementation of
features are not guaranteed until Beta mainnet.

Whereas Alpha was focused on building the base layer for the marketplace, Beta
will be focused on integrating new features. A fully stable marketplace codebase
will result in quicker releases with something new to show for almost everytime,
and it should also be a much more exciting phase for everyone than Alpha.

Beta Starts as Soon as Alpha Ends
The Beta phase of development will start immediately after the Alpha phase is
completed and moved onto mainnet. The next build that will follow the first
mainnet version of the Open Marketplace will be a Beta build and will be
released, like Alpha was up until the end, on testnet.

Two Development Branches Used During Beta
During Beta, two types of branches, Development and Stable, will be used to push
testnet builds to the community.

Development Branch

The Development branch will be the first place where new features will be
available for community testing. Because introducing new features almost
inevitably introduces bugs and issues, this may mean the Development branch
might not be super stable all the time. This is not ideal to get a good number
of testers (if it’s broken all the time, people are less interested in testing
the builds) and to show friends. After all, what’s worse than showing a friend a
build of Particl’s Open Marketplace only for it to crash in your face).

Stable Branch

This is where the Stable branch comes in. This branch will be used to push
features that have already went through a good number of tests and that have
been fixed of most bugs and issues. While the Stable branch is still a testnet
branch, it will make builds much more stable and easy to share with friends.
This branch is the final step before a feature is introduced onto mainnet.

A Feature’s Path to Mainnet
With this new strategy, a feature now has to go through one additional hoop
before being implemented on mainnet. It first needs to be tested internally by
the team, then tested within the rougher Development testnet branch. Once rid of
most of its bugs and issues, the feature gets implemented onto the Stable branch
for its final round of testing. Only after this last step will it be ready to be
integrated onto mainnet.

Biggest Milestone to Date
This mainnet release marks an important milestone for Particl. It separates it
from the group forming the majority of blockchain projects that do not have any
sort of product ready at this time and gives it an entry ticket into a much more
limited category of projects that have a product live on mainnet. What’s more,
it will signify the beginning of a brand new phase for the project and the
community as it will now be easier than ever to spend cryptocurrencies.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Want to read more about Particl and decentralized and privacy-conscious
eCommerce in general? How about this article explaining the need to
decentralize
the tech industry
[https://particl.news/the-case-for-privacy-in-the-tech-industry-87f625023d4f],
or this article breaking down how Particl could help Bitcoin succeed as a
decentralized and independent economy
[https://particl.news/bitcoin-particl-building-an-independent-economy-together-b293e214a094]
?

Maybe you want more of an update on how things are going? Then just head over to
our last Community Update
[https://particl.news/community-update-12-part-1-fe6a3f3014c2] or Particl Copay
Development Update
[https://particl.news/particl-copay-mobile-app-update-6bc8cd04f48] and get all
the information you need to stay up-to-date with everything Particl.
